title: Bittensor is a market for useful work
description: The first Bittensor Field School lesson: understand Bittensor as a market for measurable work before judging any subnet, chart or narrative.

title: Why the Bitcoin comparison helps and then breaks
description: Use Bitcoin as a starting mirror, then separate monetary security from programmable work markets.

title: Subnets are incentive machines
description: A subnet is closer to a living contest than a normal crypto project. Learn the parts before judging the story.

title: Miners are workers with an optimization problem
description: Miners do not mine hashes. They chase a scoring function that may or may not produce real value.

title: Validators are judges with capital behind them
description: Validators translate subnet work into weights. Their job is technical, economic and political at the same time.

title: Yuma turns opinions into emissions
description: Yuma Consensus is the on chain referee that converts validator weights into miner and validator rewards.

title: The weight copying problem is the hidden tax on lazy validation
description: Why validators copying each other weakens the measurement layer and why commit reveal exists.

title: Every subnet is only as good as its scoring model
description: A beautiful mission with a bad scoring model becomes a machine for rewarding the wrong behavior.

title: Multiple mechanisms let a subnet pay for more than one kind of work
description: Some subnets split emissions across different tasks. That changes how miners compete and how analysts should read performance.

title: The metagraph is the subnet truth table
description: Learn how UIDs, hotkeys, coldkeys, stake, weights and dividends appear inside a subnet state view.

title: Hotkeys and coldkeys explain who can act and who owns
description: Wallet structure matters because registration, mining, validation and ownership use different keys.

title: Root is the subnet for people who do not want to pick a subnet yet
description: Subnet zero gives subnet agnostic exposure through validators, but it has its own tradeoffs.

title: Staking is voting with exposure
description: Staking TAO into alpha pools is capital selecting which subnet deserves attention.

title: dTAO changed the center of gravity
description: Dynamic TAO moved subnet value discovery away from root judgment and toward capital flows.

title: Alpha tokens are claims on subnet specific belief
description: Each alpha token is a local market for one subnet, priced against TAO through its pool.

title: The subnet AMM makes every stake a price event
description: Bittensor subnet tokens use pools where liquidity, price and slippage shape what a position really means.

title: Slippage is the cost of being too large for the pool
description: A subnet can look cheap until your own trade moves it. Learn to think in depth, not only price.

title: Emissions are the payroll of the network
description: TAO and alpha emissions decide who gets paid, what gets reinforced and what dies quietly.

title: TaoFlow made inflow and outflow the scoreboard
description: The newer flow model rewards subnets that attract net TAO inflows and punishes negative flow.

title: Conviction is commitment with a clock attached
description: Conviction locks alpha to show longer term commitment and gives analysts a new ownership signal.

title: Subnet registration is the cost of opening a new arena
description: Anyone can create a subnet if they can pay and survive the rules. That is powerful and dangerous.

title: Deregistration is how the network makes room
description: Subnets and neurons can lose slots. Learn why scarcity of attention is built into the system.

title: Hyperparameters are the rule sheet under the scoreboard
description: Tempo, registration cost, UID limits and validator permits change how a subnet behaves.

title: A subnet GitHub can tell you what the website hides
description: Read repos for incentive code, update cadence, miner instructions and signs of real engineering.

title: A subnet without a product surface needs a harder question
description: Some subnets serve developers or validators only. Others should have a visible product. Learn the difference.

title: TAO.app is the official front door
description: Use TAO.app for explorer data, validator performance, tokenomics and Savant style discovery.

title: TaoSwap shows the market as a living table
description: Use TaoSwap data for market cap, flow, price evolution, holders and conviction across subnets.

title: TaoFlows turns staking into a tape
description: Use TaoFlows to watch real time liquidity movement instead of waiting for weekly summaries.

title: Backprop is a screener for the dTAO market
description: Use Backprop for market cap, volume, top gainers, subnet categories and trading context.

title: SubnetRadar is for risk and anomaly hunting
description: Use SubnetRadar to monitor health, conviction, signals and events that deserve follow up.

title: AlphaGap reads what investors miss between code and market
description: Use AlphaGap style thinking to connect dev updates, social velocity, wallets and valuation gaps.

title: IntoTao is the ecosystem map for newcomers
description: Use IntoTao to discover resources, tools, wallets, education and project surfaces without drowning.

title: TaoStats is the encyclopedia with an API spine
description: Use TaoStats docs, explorer, staking views and API references to cross check almost everything.

title: APIs turn Bittensor from story into evidence
description: Learn which APIs expose subnets, metagraphs, holders, transactions, slippage and social data.

title: Subnet categories are useful until they become lazy
description: LLM, compute, data, agents, media and finance subnets need different evaluation criteria.

title: LLM subnets compete on routing, quality and cost
description: Understand what to inspect in inference, model development and language related subnets.

title: Compute subnets sell capacity and reliability
description: Read GPU, serverless and infrastructure subnets through uptime, demand, utilization and pricing.

title: Data subnets live or die by provenance and demand
description: Scraping, storage and data generation subnets need buyers, quality controls and audit trails.

title: Agent subnets should be judged by tasks completed
description: Agents are narrative magnets. Learn to ask what the agent actually does, for whom and how often.

title: Media subnets turn attention into a measurable commodity
description: Bitcast, video, content and verification subnets show how Bittensor can reward media production.

title: Financial subnets need risk language, not hype language
description: Lending, trading, liquidity and prediction subnets must be read through counterparties and failure modes.

title: The red flags that make a subnet hard to trust
description: No repo, thin docs, unclear scoring, fake volume, missing product and bad incentives deserve attention.

title: A fragile subnet can still be honest
description: Learn the difference between malicious behavior, weak design, early experimentation and honest failure.

title: Validator selection is delegated judgment
description: Choosing a validator means choosing how your stake is represented across work markets.

title: Yield without context is the easiest trap in TAO
description: APY, emissions, alpha price and slippage can point in different directions.

title: Wallet flows reveal behavior before explanations do
description: Track accumulation, rotation, concentration and exits across subnet markets.

title: GitHub research is a habit more than a metric
description: Commits, releases, issues and docs should be read as evidence of shipping quality.

title: How to write a subnet thesis without fooling yourself
description: Turn product, incentives, code, flows and risk into a thesis you can update.

title: Build your Bittensor research terminal
description: Assemble the daily desk: docs, GitHub, TAO.app, TaoFlows, TaoSwap, Backprop, SubnetRadar, AlphaGap and your notes.

title: The subnet thesis exam
description: The final module gives a repeatable exam for judging any subnet from zero.
